trending.title: Meesho to infuse up to Rs 100 crore in lending arm MPPL
trending.body: E-commerce firm Meesho's board has approved a significant investment of up to Rs 100 crore in its payments subsidiary, Meesho Payments Private Limited. This capital infusion is planned through share subscriptions, including a rights issue. The funds will bolster MPPL's operations and growth. This strategic move aims to enhance MPPL's capacity to meet regulatory demands and expand its services.

trending.title: Arm forecasts higher-than-expected revenue on surging AI data center demand
trending.body: Arm Holdings anticipates higher first-quarter revenue, boosted by strong demand for its AI chip technology. However, the company has not yet secured enough supplies for a new chip, raising concerns among analysts. Despite this, Arm's designs power most smartphones globally, and its data center business shows significant growth potential.

trending.title: Microsoft may shelve 2030 clean energy target as AI lifts power use: Bloomberg News
trending.body: Microsoft may miss its 2030 renewable energy target. The company faces challenges with its ambitious climate goals. Building new data centers for artificial intelligence requires massive power. This is reshaping Microsoft's commitments. Discussions are ongoing about delaying or abandoning the renewable energy goal. The AI boom is driving demand for energy.

trending.title: Google offers changes to spam policy to avert EU antitrust fine
trending.body: Google has offered to revise its spam policy, which publishers criticized for negatively impacting their search rankings. This move aims to avoid a potential EU antitrust fine under the Digital Markets Act. The policy demoted publisher websites that featured content from commercial partners, a common monetization strategy.

trending.title: Scaling AI into production is forcing a rethink of enterprise infrastructure
trending.body: Presented by Nutanix Across industries, organizations are focused on how to move from AI pilots, proofs of concept, and cloud-based experimentation to deploying it at scale â across real workloads, for real users, in real business environments. VentureBeat spoke with Tarkan Maner, president and chief commercial officer at Nutanix, and Thomas Cornely, EVP of product management, about what that transition demands, and what it will take to get it right. âAI in general is shifting everything we do, not only in technology, but across all vertical industries, from regulated industries like banki

trending.title: Uber Q1 revenue rises 14%, slightly misses estimates; forecasts strong Q2 bookings
trending.body: Uber's Q1 revenue rose 14% year-over-year, slightly missing expectations, but the company's shares climbed on a strong forecast. This outlook signals success in its strategy of steady pricing and expansion into higher-margin areas, bolstered by international delivery demand and new geographies. The firm also anticipates robust second-quarter gross bookings and adjusted earnings per share, exceeding analyst estimates.

trending.title: Samsung reaches $1 trillion valuation: What’s fuelling the AI chip surge?
trending.body: Samsung Electronics has crossed a $1 trillion valuation. This milestone is fueled by strong demand for AI chips. The company is a major supplier of critical components for AI servers. This surge is expected to continue, with tight supply conditions likely to sustain elevated chip prices. Samsung's semiconductor division reported record profits.

trending.title: Miami startup Subquadratic claims 1,000x AI efficiency gain with SubQ model; researchers demand independent proof.
trending.body: A little-known Miami-based startup called Subquadratic emerged from stealth on Tuesday with a sweeping claim: that it has built the first large language model to fully escape the mathematical constraint that has defined â and limited â every major AI system since 2017. The company claims its first model, SubQ 1M-Preview , is the first LLM built on a fully subquadratic architecture â one where compute grows linearly with context length. If that claim holds, it would be a genuine inflection point in how AI systems scale. At 12 million tokens, the company says, its architecture reduces atte

trending.title: EU countries, lawmakers clinch provisional deal on watered-down AI rules
trending.body: EU countries and European Parliament lawmakers have agreed to a watered-down AI Act, delaying implementation for high-risk systems until December 2027. This move, aimed at reducing administrative costs and easing business competition, includes a ban on AI-generated explicit images and mandatory watermarking for AI output.

trending.title: Deeptech bets heat up; Paytm, PB Fintech, Meesho earnings
trending.body: Happy Thursday! VCs are doubling down on India’s deeptech, with early-stage bets leading the charge. This and more in today’s ETtech Morning Dispatch.

ai_agents.body: Washington and Beijing are reportedly considering official discussions on artificial intelligence. This topic may be placed on the agenda for an upcoming summit between President Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping in Beijing next week. ai_agents.title: The rapid embrace of AI in China, its biggest testing ground, may shape how AI is used globally

The nation is moving towards becoming an AI innovator.

regulatory.body: A major journal sees a 42% surge in AI-assisted scientific papers since late 2022. This rise coincides with a drop in writing quality. Researchers note the strain on peer review. The trend suggests more research, not necessarily better research, is emerging.

This device aims to move beyond current assistant-driven phones by enabling autonomous agents to handle tasks independently. The phone is expected to feature a custom MediaTek chipset with enhanced imaging and high-end hardware for faster performance and AI processing.

regulatory.title: Five architects of the AI economy explain where the wheels are coming off

regulatory.title: One command turns any open-source repo into an AI agent backdoor. OpenClaw proved no supply-chain scanner has a detection category for it
regulatory.body: Just two months ago, researchers at the Data Intelligence Lab at the University of Hong Kong introduced CLI-Anything , a new state-of-the-art tool that analyzes any repoâs source code and generates a structured command line interface (CLI) that AI coding agents can operate with a single command. Claude Code, Codex, OpenClaw, Cursor, and GitHub Copilot CLI are all supported, and since its launch in March, CLIâAnything has climbed to more than 30,000 GitHub stars. But the same mechanism that makes software agent-native opens the door to agent-level poisoning. The attack community is already

regulatory.title: Snap says $400 million AI search deal with Perplexity was called off "amicably"
regulatory.body: Snap has amicably ended its partnership with AI startup Perplexity, canceling plans to integrate Perplexity's AI search into Snapchat. The deal, valued at $400 million, would have brought conversational AI search to the app. Despite this, Snap reported stronger first-quarter results with a 12% revenue increase and narrowed net loss.

regulatory.title: Over 93,000 jobs cut in tech so far in 2026 as AI drives cuts at Freshworks, Coinbase, others
regulatory.body: Tech companies are implementing significant workforce reductions, with over 93,000 roles cut in early 2026, driven by AI and automation. Major players like Meta, Coinbase, and Freshworks are restructuring, leading to leaner teams and a shift towards efficiency.

regulatory.body: AI-skilled professionals are commanding significant pay premiums, earning up to 60% more than their peers in other tech fields. regulatory.title: Apple to pay $250M to settle lawsuit over Siri’s delayed AI features

regulatory.title: Market research is too slow for the AI era, so Brox built 60,000 identical 'digital twins' of real people you can survey instantly, repeatedly
regulatory.body: In a world where a viral TikTok video can cause a brand to trend globally in mere hours, the traditional market research cycle â often spanning 12 weeks â is becoming a liability. The lag between a survey question and the answers from a wide (or targeted) pool of respondents has become a primary bottleneck for Fortune 500 decision-makers who are forced to navigate volatile geopolitical and economic shifts with data that is frequently outdated by the time it reaches a slide deck, as industry experts have observed . gig_economy.title: Apple to invest Rs 100 crore in India renewable energy expansion
gig_economy.body: Apple is expanding its renewable energy investments in India with an initial Rs 100 crore investment in collaboration with CleanMax. This initiative will support over 150 megawatts of new renewable energy capacity, aiming to power approximately 150,000 Indian households annually.

Furthermore, Anthropic is exploring a partnership with SpaceX to develop orbital AI computing capacity.

gig_economy.body: Corning and Nvidia are partnering to significantly boost U.S. production of optical connectivity products for AI data centers.
